one_line_takeaway: A query-only, ground-truth-free adversarial attack (DGA) that steers LLM time-series forecasters toward random-walk outputs shows these models are substantially more fragile than conventional transformer forecasters under matched, imperceptible (2%-of-mean) perturbations.
key_finding: Across all five datasets, DGA degrades forecasting accuracy substantially more than GWN of identical perturbation intensity, and LLM-based forecasters (TimeGPT, LLMTime, Time-LLM) are markedly less robust than the non-LLM transformer forecasters (iTransformer, TimesNet). The most severe case is TimeGPT on Exchange, where MSE rises from 0.256 (clean) to 0.578 under DGA (GWN only reaches 0.329) and MAE from 0.368 to 0.556. LLMTime w/ GPT-4 on IstanbulTraffic jumps from MSE 0.805 (clean) / 0.883 (GWN) to 1.417 (DGA). Effects on some datasets (Weather, ETTh1) are small, but the non-LLM baselines consistently show only minor increases under DGA (e.g., iTransformer on Exchange 0.034 -> 0.049; TimesNet 0.056 -> 0.062), reinforcing that the LLM forecasters are the more vulnerable class. ACF analysis shows DGA breaks the temporal correlation structure while GWN largely preserves it.
core_contribution: The paper introduces DGA (Directional Gradient Approximation), a gradient-free, black-box, targeted adversarial attack against LLM-based time-series forecasting models. The attack's own novelty (distinct from the Related Work it cites on white-box forecasting attacks and text-based LLM attacks) is twofold: (1) it treats the forecaster as a strict black-box and cannot use ground-truth future values (to avoid information leakage), so it substitutes the model's own clean predictions as the reference and approximates a directional gradient from small random probe signals theta via finite differences; and (2) it defines the attack target explicitly as Gaussian White Noise (GWN) drawn from the input's mean/std, i.e., it steers a temporally-correlated forecast toward an i.i.d. random-walk-like output. The perturbation is applied as rho = epsilon * sign(g) under an L1/Lp-norm imperceptibility constraint, with the perturbation scale fixed at 2% of each dataset's mean value.
threat_model: Explicitly stated in Section 3.2. The attacker perturbs the historical input window of a deployed LLM-based forecaster to make its forecast deviate maximally from both its normal output and the true future. Attacker capabilities: (a) NO access to training data, (b) NO access to the model's internal parameters/architecture (strict black-box, so no white-box gradients), (c) NO access to ground-truth future values, and (d) the ONLY capability is query access to the target model. Because true future values are unavailable at attack time, the optimization objective replaces ground truth Y with the model's own predicted values Y-hat. The imperceptibility budget is an Lp-norm bound epsilon set to 2% of the dataset mean.
limitations: (1) Evaluation is univariate only, at a single fixed horizon (96->48). (2) Absolute degradations are modest on several cells (Weather MSE moves ~0.001; ETTh1 changes are small), so the dramatic framing rests mainly on Exchange and IstanbulTraffic and on relative comparison to GWN. (3) The 'ground-truth-free' claim is realized by substituting the model's own clean predictions, which still requires clean query access to produce the reference before attacking. (4) Time-LLM is only tested with a GPT-2 backbone, weaker than the GPT-3.5/4 backbones used elsewhere, complicating cross-model comparison. (5) Defenses are only discussed conceptually and never implemented or measured, so the paper demonstrates a vulnerability but not any mitigation. (6) No transferability or query-cost analysis is reported.

quantitative_results: TimeGPT on Exchange: MSE clean 0.256 / GWN 0.329 / DGA 0.578; MAE clean 0.368 / DGA 0.556
LLMTime w/ GPT-4 on IstanbulTraffic: MSE clean 0.805 / GWN 0.883 / DGA 1.417; MAE clean 0.779 / DGA 1.214
LLMTime w/ GPT-3.5 on ETTh2: MSE clean 0.263 / GWN 0.263 / DGA 0.275; MAE clean 0.372 / DGA 0.408
LLMTime w/ Mistral on Exchange: MSE clean 0.151 / GWN 0.160 / DGA 0.219
Non-LLM robustness on Exchange under DGA: iTransformer MSE 0.034 -> 0.049; TimesNet MSE 0.056 -> 0.062
Perturbation budget fixed at 2% of each dataset's mean value; input window 96 steps, forecast horizon 48 steps
Data split: 50% train / 25% validation / 25% test; attacker accesses only test input
baselines_compared: Gaussian White Noise (GWN) of matched perturbation intensity (primary control for attack effectiveness)
Non-LLM forecasters iTransformer and TimesNet (robustness comparison against LLM-based models)
Target LLM forecasters attacked: TimeGPT (pretrained time-series LLM), LLMTime with GPT-3.5 / GPT-4 / LLaMa 2 / Mistral backbones, Time-LLM with GPT-2
